channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Press Freedom Rankings Show Southern Africa Faces Persistent Challenges
item.description: According to the latest Reporters Without Borders World Press Freedom Index, Southern Africa continues to face challenges in media freedom, even as some countries show modest improvements. South Afric...

item.title: Tanzania Introduces Special Guidelines to Reintegrate Experienced Journalists
item.description: Tanzania government imeanzisha mpango maalum wa kuruhusu waandishi wa habari waliokuwa wametupwa nje kwa sababu ya mfumo wa ithibati (accreditation) kurudi kwenye taaluma rasmi ya uandishi wa habari. ...

item.title: Timothée Chalamet chooses Knicks playoff game over attending the Met Gala with Kylie Jenner
item.description: Timothée Chalamet opted to attend the New York Knicks’ playoff game against the Philadelphia 76ers rather than accompany his girlfriend Kylie Jenner at the Met Gala. This marks a repeat of last year’s...

item.title: Woman arrested for alleged fraud involving multiple victims
item.description: Authorities have arrested a woman accused of defrauding her childhood friend, and more victims are reportedly coming forward. According to reports from the Philippine National Police (PNP) and the Cri...

item.title: Sake Manila 2026 Returns to Okada Manila with New Sake Labels
item.description: Sake Manila, the much-awaited celebration of Japanese sake, is returning this May at Okada Manila. The event promises to showcase an expanded selection of sake labels, offering enthusiasts and newcome...

item.title: Doma United and Sporting Lagos secure narrow wins in NNL Super 4 opener
item.description: For the opening day of the Nigeria National League (NNL) Super 4 at Remo Stars Stadium in Ikenne, Ogun State, Doma United and Sporting Lagos FC both managed narrow 1-0 victories. Sporting Lagos faced ...

item.title: Passengers isolated on MV Hondius after hantavirus outbreak as evacuation plans remain unclear
item.description: Passengers aboard the MV Hondius, a polar cruise ship off the coast of West Africa, remain in isolation following a hantavirus outbreak that has resulted in three deaths and several serious illnesses....

item.title: CEOs anticipate investment in AI and infrastructure to sustain economic growth amid global uncertainty
item.description: Top global CEOs and investment leaders are maintaining an optimistic outlook despite ongoing geopolitical tensions, including the war in Iran. Speaking at the Milken Institute Global Conference in Bev...

item.title: Suluhu Calls on Ruto to Address Youth Activism and Regional Stability
item.description: Tanzanian President Samia Suluhu Hassan has urged Kenyan President William Ruto to take a strong stance on what she described as disorderly conduct by Gen Z activists. Speaking during the Kenya-Tanzan...

item.title: Corrections officer pleads not guilty to assault charge during disputed strip search at ACT jail
item.description: A corrections officer at the Alexander Maconochie Centre in Canberra has pleaded not guilty to a common assault charge following an allegedly unlawful strip search of a prisoner in May 2023. Sean Alex...

item.title: Muturi Condemns Political Attacks on Former President Uhuru Kenyatta
item.description: Democratic Party leader Justin Muturi has openly criticised allies of President William Ruto for targeting former President Uhuru Kenyatta with repeated attacks. In a strong statement, Muturi describe...

item.title: Sofronio and Ryan Cayabyab Collaborate for MaestroClass at Proscenium Theater
item.description: The MaestroClass series, a much-anticipated musical event, is bringing together renowned Filipino composer Ryan Cayabyab and talented singer Sofronio Vasquez. Set to take place at the Proscenium Theat...

item.title: B.C. experiences record-breaking temperatures across multiple regions over the weekend
item.description: Over the past weekend, British Columbia experienced unusually warm weather, breaking daily temperature records in at least 24 locations. Several areas, including Pemberton, Osoyoos, Lillooet, and Cach...

item.title: Local elections focus on road maintenance as potholes remain a major concern
item.description: As voters prepare for the English local elections, road conditions, particularly potholes, are emerging as a key issue alongside the cost of living, healthcare, and crime. Potholes pose a financial ri...

item.title: Critical CopyFail Vulnerability Discovered in Linux, Poses Risk to Enterprise Systems
item.description: A serious security flaw, dubbed 'CopyFail' and tracked as CVE-2026-31431, has been discovered in Linux kernel versions 7.0 and earlier, affecting nearly all modern Linux distributions. Security resear...

item.title: Major Investments Drive Tourism Growth and Infrastructure Expansion in Bahir Dar
item.description: Bahir Dar, mji mkuu wa eneo la Amhara nchini Ethiopia, unaingia katika hatua mpya ya maendeleo inayochochewa na sekta ya utalii, kufuatia uwekezaji mkubwa na miradi ya kisasa inayoendelea kujengwa. Na...

item.title: PTI leaders express concerns over new Punjab coordination committee
item.description: The Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf (PTI) has seen renewed internal tensions following the creation of a Punjab Coordination and Monitoring Committee. Notified by party secretary general Salman Akram Raja, t...

item.title: 12-year-old girl dies in North West floods as heavy rains cause widespread damage
item.description: A tragic incident in the North West province has left a community reeling after a 12-year-old girl was swept away by floodwaters following heavy rainfall. The child was reportedly playing outside with...

item.title: U.S. and Iran Escalate Military Tensions in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: On May 4, 2026, tensions between the United States and Iran escalated in the Gulf as both countries engaged in military actions around the Strait of Hormuz, a critical chokepoint for global energy tra...

item.title: Karachi to experience hot but slightly cooler weather with temperatures under 40°C
item.description: KARACHI: The Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) has forecast that Karachi will continue to experience hot and dry weather for the next 24 hours, with maximum temperatures likely to range between...
